title: CLAUDE.md
text: mizerEcopath bridges Ecopath (biomass-pool ecosystem models) mizer (size-spectrum models). goal initialise calibrate mizer models using Ecopath parameters (B, Q, P, C, DC) matching size-integrated mizer quantities Ecopath counterparts.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: newAllometricParams() — create allometric (power-law) MizerParams object starting point addEcopathParams() — attach Ecopath-derived parameters species_params matchEcopath() — master calibration: calls matchConsumption() matchProduction() iteratively matchCatch() — adjust gear selectivity catchability match observed catch size distributions (uses TMB optimisation, see src/objective_function.cpp) matchDiet() — set predation preferences match diet matrix alignResource() — scale resource spectrum match fish abundance tuneEcopath() — interactive Shiny gadget manual fine-tuning

title: CLAUDE.md
text: functions take MizerParams object return modified MizerParams (functional style). “Ecopath quantities” (B, Q/B, P/B, catch) computed integrating mizer size spectrum — see getConsumption(), getSomaticProduction(), getGonadicProduction(), getProduction(), getM0B(), getM2B(), getZB(), getDietMatrix(). matchExtMortOnce(), matchGonadicProportionOnce(), matchRespirationOnce() single-step helpers called iteratively higher-level matching functions. bindParams() combines multiple single-species MizerParams multi-species model.

title: CLAUDE.md
text: separate mathematical framework (PDE-based) models cohort growth diffusion fitting age--size data: - project_diffusion() → solve_diffusion_pde() → solve_double_sweep() (numerical PDE solver) - simulateCohort() single-cohort dynamics - plotAgeLikelihood(), getLogLik() age-data likelihood fitting - Supporting biology: age_mat_vB(), von_mises_pdf(), spawning_density(), length_rebinning_matrix()

Using general result \[ \int e^{ax}\exp\left(-\frac{(x-\mu)^2}{2\sigma^2}\right)dx = \sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{\sigma^2}}\exp\left(\mu+\frac{^2\sigma^2}{2}\right) \tag{7}\] \(= 2-\lambda\) \(\mu = \log(w/w_0)-\log(\beta)\) find \[ \begin{split} I_g&= \alpha\, w_0^{2-\lambda}\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{\sigma^2}}\exp\left((2-\lambda)(\log(w/w_0)-\log(\beta))+\frac{(2-\lambda)^2\sigma^2}{2}\right)\\ &=\alpha w^{2-\lambda}\sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{\sigma^2}}\beta^{\lambda - 2 + \lambda\) obtain \[ g(w) = \left((1-f)\gamma \alpha \sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{\sigma^2}}\beta^{\lambda - 2}\exp\left(\frac{(2-\lambda)^2\sigma^2}{2}\right)-k_s\right) w^n. \tag{9}\] assume metabolic loss fraction \(f_c/f\) incoming energy (refer \(f_c\) critical feeding level) simplifies \[ g(w) =\left( \left(1-\frac{f_c}{f}\right)(1-f)\gamma \alpha \sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{\sigma^2}}\beta^{\lambda - 2}\exp\left(\frac{(2-\lambda)^2\sigma^2}{2}\right)\right) w^n. \tag{10}\] can evaluate diffusion rate \(d(w)\) manner. extra factor \(w_p\) changes \(\) \(2-\lambda\) \(3-\lambda\) obtain \[ d(w) = \left((1-f(w))\gamma \alpha^2 \sqrt{\frac{2\pi}{\sigma^2}}\beta^{\lambda - 3)\sigma^2}{2}\right). \tag{12}\] get feel typical magnitude factor let’s consider concrete values \[ (1-f_c/f)=0.6, \qquad\alpha = 0.8, \qquad\beta = 100, \qquad\sigma = 2, \qquad\lambda = 2. \tag{13}\] \[ d(w) \approx 0.15 g(w)w. \tag{14}\] However see value \(d(w)\) strongly influenced width feeding kernel. choose \(\sigma = 1\) \(d(w)\approx 0.03 g(w)w\). decrease diffusion rate decreasing \(\sigma\) may explains result (Datta, Delius, Law, Plank 2010) stability system decreases decreasing \(\sigma\).

text: derive double-sweep method (Thomas’ algorithm) solving tridiagonal linear system. system given \[ x = b \tag{46}\] \(\) tridiagonal matrix lower diagonal \(L\), main diagonal \(D\), upper diagonal \(U\), components system takes form \[ L_ix_{-1} +D_ix_{} + U_ix_{+1} - b_i = 0 \tag{47}\] \(=1,\dots,N\) understanding \(L_1 = U_N = 0\) interior points \(=1,\dots,N\) solved . boundary conditions encoded choice \(b_1\) \(b_N\). Thomas algorithm proceeds making Ansatz \[ x_{-1}=\alpha_ix_i+\beta_i, \tag{48}\] \(\alpha_i\) \(\beta_i\) determined. Substituting Eq. 48 Eq. 43 gives \[ U_ix_{+1}+(\alpha_iL_i+D_i)x_i+\beta_iL_i - b_i = 0. \tag{49}\] Similarly using \(x_{}=\alpha_{+1}x_{+1}+\beta_{+1}\) Eq. 49 gives \[ \left[U_i + (\alpha_iL_i+D_i)\alpha_{+1}\right]x_{+1}+\left[(\alpha_iL_i+D_i)\beta_{+1}+\beta_iL_i-b_i\right]=0. \tag{50}\] can satisfy making expressions square brackets vanish, gives us \[ \alpha_{+1}=-\frac{U_i}{\alpha_iL_i+D_i},\qquad \beta_{+1}=-\frac{\beta_iL_i-b_i}{\alpha_iL_i+D_i}. \tag{51}\] Looking case \(=1\) definition \(L_1=0\), see \(\alpha_2=-U_1/D_1\) \(\beta_2=b_1/D_1\). can now use Eq. 51 determine \(\alpha\)’s \(\beta\)’s. consider Eq. 49 \(=N\) use \(U_N=0\), find \[ (\alpha_NL_N+D_N)x_N+\beta_NL_N - b_N = 0, \] get \[ x_N = \frac{b_N - \beta_N L_N}{\alpha_N L_N + D_N}. \] can now use \(\alpha\)’s \(\beta\)’s calculate \(x_{N-1}\), \(x_{N-2}\), …, \(x_1\) back substitution step. implemented function solve_double_sweep().

code: params <- validParams(celtic_params) #> Warning in validParams(celtic_params): Your MizerParams object was created with #> an earlier version of mizer. You can upgrade it with `params <- #> validParams(params)` where you should replace `params` by the name of the #> variable that holds your MizerParams object. species <- "Herring" w <- params@w  # Without diffusion params_no_diffusion <- params ps_no_diffusion <- mizer::steadySingleSpecies(params_no_diffusion, species) n_steady_no_diffusion <- ps_no_diffusion@initial_n[species, ]  # Set nonzero diffusion rate species_params(params)$d_over_g <- 0.15  # Set diffusion from d_over_g # d(w) = d_over_g * g(w) * w for (s in species_params(params)$species) {     d_over_g <- species_params(params)[s, "d_over_g"]     w <- params@w     growth <- getEGrowth(params)[s, ]     n <- params@species_params[s, "n"]     g_0 <- growth[1] / w[1]^n     d_0 <- d_over_g * g_0     diffusion(params)[s, ] <- d_0 * w^(n + 1) }  # Solve for steady state using the upwind scheme ps <- mizer::steadySingleSpecies(params, species) n_steady <- ps@initial_n[species, ]  # Plot the resulting abundance density versus size plot(w, n_steady, log = "xy", type = "l", lwd = 2,      xlab = "Weight (g)", ylab = "Abundance density",      main = paste("Steady state solution for", species)) lines(w, n_steady_no_diffusion, col = "red", lwd = 2, lty = 2) p0 <- params p0@initial_n[species, ] <- n_steady_no_diffusion y0 <- plotYieldVsSize(p0, species = species, x_var = "Length",                       return_data = TRUE)[[1]] |>     dplyr::filter(Type == "Model catch") y0$Type <- "No diffusion"  p <- params p@initial_n[species, ] <- n_steady y <- plotYieldVsSize(p, species = species, x_var = "Length",                      return_data = TRUE)[[1]] |>     dplyr::filter(Type == "Model catch") y$Type <- "With diffusion" y <- rbind(y0, y)  ggplot(y, aes(x = l, y = `Catch density`, colour = Type)) +     geom_line()

title: Extending an Ecopath model to a mizer model
text: typical workflow creating mizer model Ecopath data involves following steps: Export Ecopath Parameters: Open Ecopath model “Ecopath Ecosim” export parameters CSV files. Prepare Species Table: Create table R one row species want include mizer model. table must contain: species: name species. w_max: largest observed size species. w_mat: weight maturity. ecopath_groups: corresponding Ecopath group(s). Initialize Model: Use newAllometricParams() create mizer model based parameters. sets model allometric encounter mortality rates, initially interactions. r     params <- newAllometricParams(species_params) Match Observations: Use various functions provided package matchBiomass() match total biomass species, matchGrowth() match growth rates, matchCatch() match total yield size distribution catch, matchConsumption() match total consumption species. Tune Model: Use interactive Shiny gadget tuneEcopath() fine-tune model reproduce observed size structure Ecopath parameters. r     params <- tuneEcopath(params) gadget allows interactively adjust parameters see effects growth, yield, consumption real-time. Set predation preferences: Use matchDiet() set predation preferences model match observed diet compositions. r     params <- matchDiet(params)

title: Extending an Ecopath model to a mizer model

title: Get diffusion rate — getDiffusion
text: diffusion rate \(d_i(w)\) species \(\) weight \(w\) contributions encounter fish prey resource. determined summing prey species resource spectrum integrating prey sizes \(w_p\), weighted predation kernel \(\phi(w,w_p)\): $$ d_i(w) = (1-f_i(w))(\alpha_i(1-\psi_i(w)))^2\gamma_i(w) \int \left( \theta_{ip} N_R(w_p) + \sum_{j} \theta_{ij} N_j(w_p) \right) \phi_i(w,w_p) w_p^2 \, dw_p. $$ \(N_j(w)\) abundance density species \(j\) \(N_R(w)\) abundance density resource. overall prefactor \(\gamma_i(w)\) determines predation power predator. interpreted search volume set mizer::setSearchVolume() function. predation kernel \(\phi(w,w_p)\) set mizer::setPredKernel() function. species interaction matrix \(\theta_{ij}\) set mizer::setInteraction() resource interaction vector \(\theta_{ip}\) taken interaction_resource column params@species_params. \(f(w)\) feeding level calculated mizer::getFeedingLevel(). \(\psi(w)\) proportion available energy invested reproduction instead growth, stored params@psi.

text: Unlike getMetabolicRespiration(), function follows Ecopath definition respiration rate assimilated food used production. expressed first master equation Ecopath: $$Q = P + R + U$$ \(Q\) consumption rate, \(P\) production rate, \(R\) respiration rate, \(U\) unassimilated part consumption rate, .e., \(U = (1 - \alpha) Q\), \(\alpha\) assimilation efficiency. title: Match the model's production rate to empirical observations/estimates — matchProduction
text: Ecopath explicitly account loss due gonad production. However energy invested gonad production lost due inefficiency reproductive process. Therefore mizer need include loss respiration rate. Thus $$R = K + G - R_dd * w_0$$ \(K\) metabolic rate, \(G\) gonadic production rate, \(R_dd\) rate offspring production, \(w_0\) weight offspring size enters model. Thus \(G - R_dd * w_0\) net loss due gonad production. matchProduction function calls matchProductionOnce() repeatedly production matches within tolerance specified tol parameter. function return warning maximum number iterations reached without converging.
